query_facts.fact_identified: In 2020, AI struggled to solve basic math problems reliably, but by 2025 it could compete in the International Math Olympiad and achieve a gold medal-level performance.
query_facts.feedback: AI models from OpenAI and DeepMind achieved gold-medal level performance at the 2025 International Mathematical Olympiad, solving 5 out of 6 problems.

query_facts.fact_identified: AI is already widely used by Singaporeans, including for travel planning where it can recommend destinations and generate personalised itineraries.
query_facts.feedback: AI tools like TripIt and Sygic Travel are used in Singapore to create personalised travel itineraries.

query_facts.fact_identified: In Singapore's civil service, AI is used in agency call centres to transcribe conversations in all four official languages and generate summaries so officers can focus on serving the public.
query_facts.feedback: CPF Board uses AI to transcribe and summarise calls, allowing call agents to focus on assisting callers.

query_facts.fact_identified: Singapore's ability to combine technology and human expertise attracts high-quality investments and creates good jobs.
query_facts.feedback: Singapore's Economic Development Board works with companies and government to prepare and upskill Singaporeans for new opportunities.

query_facts.fact_identified: AI adoption is not limited to large companies, SMEs can also benefit.
query_facts.feedback: Singapore SMEs are supported to adopt generative AI through initiatives and ecosystem support.

query_facts.fact_identified: AI is being combined with robotics and autonomous machines at Tuas Port.
query_facts.feedback: Tuas Port operates a fully automated container terminal with autonomous electric vehicles coordinated by AI.

query_facts.fact_identified: AI is studied for automation at Changi Airport.
query_facts.feedback: Changi Airport has deployed autonomous tractors and uses AI in initiatives to increase productivity.

query_facts.fact_identified: GE Vernova's turbine repair facility in Singapore uses AI to detect anomalies in turbine components and alert human operators for precise diagnosis.
query_facts.feedback: GE Vernova's Singapore facility uses AI-powered automated inspection systems to enhance turbine repair.

query_facts.fact_identified: SMEs like Q&M Dental use AI to analyse dental X-rays to help detect fillings, impacted teeth, and tooth decay more accurately.
query_facts.feedback: Q&M Dental uses AI models to analyze dental X-rays for various dental conditions.

query_facts.fact_identified: In the 1980s, Singapore launched a nationwide computerisation drive that equipped students and workers with computer skills and transformed the economy.
query_facts.feedback: Singapore's Civil Service Computerisation Programme in the 1980s encouraged ICT adoption and skills development.

query_facts.fact_identified: When the internet arrived in the 1990s, Singapore successfully adopted it and propelled the nation forward.
query_facts.feedback: Singapore established its first internet connection in 1990 and grew broadband penetration rapidly.

query_facts.fact_identified: At PSA, crane operators were retrained to remotely supervise and manage multiple cranes and vehicles, boosting productivity and improving job quality.
query_facts.feedback: PSA retrained crane operators to manage cranes remotely, enhancing productivity and job quality.

query_facts.fact_identified: The SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme provides Singaporeans aged 40 and above with $4,000 in SkillsFuture credits and training allowances of up to $3,000 per month for up to 24 months for full-time study.
query_facts.feedback: The SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme offers $4,000 credit top-up and training allowances for mid-career Singaporeans.

query_facts.fact_identified: Vinoth Nanda Kumaran, aged 41 in 2025, transitioned from private hire driver to Business Intelligence Developer after using SkillsFuture credits to pursue a diploma in data engineering and continuing his studies in Business Analytics at SUSS.
query_facts.feedback: Vinoth Nanda Kumaran transitioned careers with SkillsFuture support and is now a Business Intelligence Developer.
